question_html:
Write down the appropriate preposition for the following sentences:

(a) The nurse waited _____ the patient.

(b) We are related _____ marriage.

(c) He is vulnerable _____ criticism.

(d) We should not yield _____ any pressure.

(e) The passport will be valid _____ one year.
solution_html:
(a) on/for (b) to/by (c) to (d) to (e) for

Marks Distribution:

For writing the appropriate prepositions; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Complete the following sentences:

(a) It was many days since I ______.

(b) I went to the market yesterday. I went there with a view to ______.

(c) I found a box near the door. The box was too large for ______.

(d) I attended a concert last night. Had you come with me, ______.

(e) I spent some wonderful years in my village. I wish ______.
solution_html:
(a) had seen my best friend

(b) buying some fresh vegetables

(c) me to lift alone

(d) you would/could/might have enjoyed it a lot

(e) I could go back and live there again/I were there.

Marks Distribution:

For completing the sentences using the correct words; (0.5 ×5)=2.5 marks.

[N.B: Any kind of sentence that is correct, will also get allocated marks.]

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Sustainable Urban Development’.
solution_html:
Sustainable Urban Development

Sustainable urban development means building cities that meet present needs without harming future generations. With rapid urbanization, especially in countries like Bangladesh, cities face problems such as traffic congestion, pollution, waste mismanagement, and shortage of housing. Sustainable development seeks to balance economic growth, environmental protection, and social well-being. It promotes eco-friendly buildings, renewable energy, efficient public transport, and proper waste recycling. Green spaces, clean water, and affordable housing are also essential for a livable city. In Dhaka, unplanned growth has created serious challenges, but sustainable policies can improve the situation. If cities are planned wisely, they will be inclusive, safe, and resilient. Therefore, sustainable urban development is crucial for a healthier and more prosperous future.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the title “Sustainable Urban Development”0.5 mark

For defining

For mentioning urban challenges01 mark

For explaining key aspects1.5 marks

For giving benefits & conclusion1 mark

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
